description_long: This object is a modern, twin-engine jet fighter aircraft with a sleek, angular design and a low-profile fuselage. The wings are sharply swept back and feature pronounced leading-edge extensions, contributing to its aerodynamic efficiency and agility. The twin vertical stabilizers are canted outward, and the tail section includes two large exhaust nozzles, indicating powerful jet propulsion. The cockpit is positioned forward on the fuselage and is enclosed by a tinted canopy, providing visibility for the pilot. The landing gear is tricycle-type, with a single nose wheel and two main wheels visible beneath the wings. The overall surface is finished in a light gray color with darker accents along the panel lines and control surfaces.
description: A sleek, twin-engine jet fighter with swept-back wings, twin vertical stabilizers, and a forward-mounted enclosed cockpit. The surface is light gray with dark accents and features tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Rear view showing twin jet exhausts, canted twin vertical stabilizers, and the broad, angular wings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the forward cockpit, swept-back wings, and visible tricycle landing gear.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the pointed nose, canopy, and the full span of the swept wings with leading-edge extensions.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the cockpit, wing configuration, and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, sharply swept wings with pronounced leading-edge extensions and slight anhedral angle; no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted at the rear fuselage with large exhaust nozzles.
attribute_tail_design: Twin canted vertical stabilizers with a conventional horizontal stabilizer; H-tail configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with one nose wheel and two main gear units, all retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a bubble canopy integrated into the fuselage.
